title: xz-utils backdoor
subtitle: liblzma supply-chain implant
query: Assess CVE-2024-3094 in xz-utils / liblzma and tell me how urgently to act.

data.query: Assess CVE-2024-3094 in xz-utils / liblzma and tell me how urgently to act.

data.summary: CVE-2024-3094 is a CVSS 10.0 supply-chain backdoor planted in xz-utils / liblzma 5.6.0–5.6.1. The malicious build-process injection can intercept liblzma calls for any linked software, enabling potential unauthenticated RCE via OpenSSH on glibc Linux. EPSS probability is 86% (99.7th percentile) — top 0.3% of all scored CVEs.
data.recommended_action: SSVC: Act — downgrade xz-utils/liblzma to 5.4.x immediately (last clean release); do not wait for a maintenance window. Audit all Linux systems for xz 5.6.0/5.6.1 (Fedora 41, Debian testing/sid, Arch, openSUSE Tumbleweed were affected). Restart sshd after downgrade; rotate SSH host keys. Treat impacted systems as potentially compromised and initiate incident response.

cves.summary: Deliberate supply-chain backdoor in xz-utils/liblzma 5.6.0–5.6.1. A prebuilt malicious object injected at build time intercepts liblzma calls for all linked software, enabling potential unauthenticated RCE via OpenSSH on glibc Linux systems.

reasoning_chain: cve_lookup(CVE-2024-3094) -> CVSS 10.0 CRITICAL, CWE-506 (Embedded Malicious Code), xz 5.6.0 and 5.6.1 affected, deliberate supply-chain backdoor targeting liblzma
exploit_check(CVE-2024-3094) -> has_public_exploit=false (no Exploit-DB or GitHub PoC; backdoor requires specific triggering conditions)
kev_check(CVE-2024-3094) -> in_catalog=false (not in CISA KEV at query time)
epss_score(CVE-2024-3094) -> probability=0.860, percentile=0.997 — top 0.3% of all scored CVEs
patch_lookup(CVE-2024-3094) -> no NVD CPE fix entries; remediation is downgrade to xz 5.4.x per vendor advisories
attack_mapping([CWE-506]) -> no curated ATT&CK mapping returned for Embedded Malicious Code
Decision: CVSS 10.0 + 86% EPSS + purposeful backdoor = Act urgency despite absence from CISA KEV

ssvc.rationale: SSVC decision Attend: CVE-2024-3094 EPSS predicts high near-term exploitation likelihood.
